methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $262M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Outrider, a Series D robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $262M to date, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). The peer set includes Exotec, Divergent, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics, spanning Series C through Series E.

Two valuation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.5x multiplier typical of Series D advancement, implies $393M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.8B, with a distribution ranging from $1.0B (Nimble Robotics, Series C) to $2.3B (Divergent, Series E).

Triangulation of both methods produces a valuation range of $334M to $2.1B, with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding Outrider's positioning within the peer cohort. Stage-progression methodology substantially underweights relative to peer multiples, suggesting either conservative capital deployment or valuation compression versus comparable-stage competitors. The peer-median approach, anchored to recent market transactions, is more credible given market data. Key sensitivities include Outrider's revenue scale relative to peers, unit economics, and near-term funding environment conditions in the robotics sector.
